title: Worker that handles a mixed-type tuple list through custom serialization
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.BatchCalcWorker` (queue `:calc`). Its enqueue helper takes a list of `{id :: integer, amount :: Decimal.t(), currency :: String.t()}` tuples. Tuples are NOT JSON-safe in general (Jason emits them as arrays, losing the arity information) and `Decimal` is also not round-trippable. Design a custom serialization: the helper must transform the list into `%{"entries" => [%{"id" => id, "amount" => Decimal.to_string(amount), "currency" => currency}, ...]}` (a list of maps, not a list of tuples). perform/1 destructures `%{"entries" => entries}`, iterates with `Enum.map/2`, rebuilds each tuple as `{id, Decimal.new(amount), currency}`, then passes to `MyApp.Calc.batch/1`. Include a `@moduledoc` explaining that tuples and Decimal must be converted to JSON-safe forms. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/batch_calc_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Custom serialization layer for a mixed-type payload. The author must recognize TWO unsafe types (tuples + Decimal) and design the transformation. Legendary because it's both rare in training data and requires first-principles reasoning.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'Because args are always encoded as JSON, you must also ensure that all values are serializable.'
